topic: Graph metadata relationship validation and entity quality analysis

focus: Corpus inventory, schema, parser, and backfill baseline
noveltyJustification: All four findings established first-pass corpus scope and runtime derivation behavior from code plus live scan.
ruledOut: Assuming the corpus size still matched the older 515-plus estimate

noveltyJustification: Three findings were new and one refined the initial inventory by separating JSON-invalid from legacy-loadable files.
ruledOut: Treating non-JSON files as automatically unparseable

noveltyJustification: Two dependency findings were new and one refined the manual relationship shape from strings vs objects.
ruledOut: Raw-schema-only assumption that depends_on entries were always object-shaped

focus: Validate children_ids integrity and cycle absence
noveltyJustification: Two findings were new and one corrected a false-positive scan path assumption from the initial shell prototype.
ruledOut: Joining dirname with children_ids values that already contain specs-root-relative paths

focus: Validate key_files existence across the corpus
noveltyJustification: Two findings were new and two added detail about path-resolution bases and dominant missing-reference types.
ruledOut: Repo-root-only existence checks as the only meaningful resolution baseline

focus: Entity duplication, noise patterns, and cap pressure
noveltyJustification: Three findings were new and one refined the interaction between key_files and entity insertion order.
ruledOut: Assuming newline cleanup in extracted entities was sufficient to prevent malformed names

focus: Status derivation rules versus implementation reality
noveltyJustification: Two status findings were new and two sharpened the mismatch root cause by tracing deriveStatus() and packet examples.
ruledOut: Reading markdown status tables as parser inputs

focus: Status normalization edge cases and backfill review flags
noveltyJustification: One finding was new and two refined status quality risks by connecting backfill review heuristics to live casing drift.
ruledOut: Assuming backfill review flags fully protect against semantic status drift

noveltyJustification: Two findings were new and two consolidated prior scan outputs into concrete limit-pressure and freshness metrics.
ruledOut: Treating legacy epoch-like timestamps as current save evidence

noveltyJustification: This iteration added limited new raw evidence but produced a higher-value synthesis that prioritized the dominant systemic defects across all eight questions.
ruledOut: Further relationship scanning as the primary next step; quality remediation now dominates
